# plugin_wire — End-to-end plugin agent wiring test
#
# Verifies that a plugin with an agent definition is correctly discovered,
# loaded, and merged into the orchestrator agent list. The plugin manifest
# uses the current TOML schema (description, provider, timeout, etc.) to
# ensure the test exercises the modern format rather than the deprecated
# v2.3.0 minimal shape.

env SIN_CODE_CONFIG_DIR=$WORK/config
mkdir -p $WORK/config/sin-code/plugins
mkdir -p $WORK/source-plugin/bin

# Write the plugin manifest using the current schema (post-v2.3.0).
# printf + shell-quoted escapes (no heredoc) keeps the testscript parser happy.
sin-code execute --command 'printf "name = \"hello-plugin\"\nversion = \"1.0.0\"\ndescription = \"A sample plugin for E2E testing\"\nauthor = \"SIN-Code\"\nlicense = \"MIT\"\nmin_sin_code = \"2.0.0\"\ncapabilities = [\"agent\"]\n\n[[subcommand]]\nname = \"greet\"\ndescription = \"Say hello\"\nbinary = \"./bin/greet\"\n\n[[agents]]\nname = \"fastcoder\"\ntype = \"code\"\nmodel = \"openai/gpt-4o\"\nprovider = \"openai\"\n\n[[tools]]\nname = \"shout\"\ndescription = \"Shout a name\"\nbinary = \"./bin/shout\"\nargs = [\"name\"]\ntimeout = 10\n" > $WORK/source-plugin/plugin.toml'
stdout 'Exit:'

# Plugin binaries.
sin-code execute --command 'printf "#!/bin/sh\necho hi\n" > $WORK/source-plugin/bin/greet && chmod +x $WORK/source-plugin/bin/greet'
stdout 'Exit:'
sin-code execute --command 'printf "#!/bin/sh\necho shouting\n" > $WORK/source-plugin/bin/shout && chmod +x $WORK/source-plugin/bin/shout'
stdout 'Exit:'

# Install into the discovered plugin dir. Use the real cp via execute
# because testscript's own cp requires the destination dir to pre-exist.
sin-code execute --command 'cp -r $WORK/source-plugin $WORK/config/sin-code/plugins/hello-plugin'
stdout 'Exit:'

# plugin list still works
sin-code plugin --path $WORK/config/sin-code/plugins list
stdout 'hello-plugin'

# plugin agent is merged into orchestrator-agents and tagged [plugin X]
sin-code orchestrator-agents --agents-dir $WORK/none
stdout 'plugin-hello-plugin-fastcoder'
stdout '\[plugin hello-plugin\]'

# plugin agent is also discoverable as JSON
sin-code orchestrator-agents --agents-dir $WORK/none --format json
stdout 'plugin-hello-plugin-fastcoder'
